As airport manager, CAG performs the key functions of airport operations, air hub development, retail and commercial activities, infrastructure development and airport emergency services. Innovation & Technology: Incorporate the latest innovations in automation and transportation technologies to create a world-class APM system, enhancing the passenger experience and operational efficiency at T5.   Regulatory Compliance: Ensure the APM system meets all relevant international and local regulations, standards, and safety requirements throughout the project lifecycle.   Progress Monitoring: Track the progress of the project, proactively identify risks or delays, and implement appropriate mitigation strategies to keep the project on track.   Documentation & Reporting: Maintain thorough documentation and provide regular project updates to senior management and stakeholders.   Risk Management: Identify and address potential risks or challenges that may arise during the project execution phase. Up to 5 years of relevant experience in APM systems or rail transportation systems is preferred.   Strong analytical skills with a keen eye for detail and a problem-solving mindset.   Excellent verbal and written communication skills to effectively coordinate with teams and stakeholders.   Ability to multitask and thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.   Highly motivated, independent, and a strong team player.   Familiarity with project management software and tools is an advantage.   A proactive attitude and the ability to take ownership of tasks and projects.  Candidates with no experience are welcome to apply.

Company description:Singapore LNG Corporation Pte Ltd (SLNG) was incorporated by the Energy Market Authority of Singapore in June 2009 to build, own and operate Singapore's very first open-access, multi-user LNG Terminal. This is a key national infrastructure that supports Singapore's energy diversification strategy and future economic development in the energy sector. With more than 95% of electricity in Singapore being generated using natural gas, the SLNG Terminal helps to enhance the country's energy security by enabling natural gas to be shipped to Singapore from anywhere in the world. It also serves as a platform to facilitate the development of new LNG-related businesses, thereby contributing to the growth of Singapore's energy industry and the creation of new job opportunities.Job description:ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES  Provide technical/maintenance support and solutions to the terminal operation Teams including supporting the development of maintenance strategy focusing on equipment reliability, compliance, spares inventory requirement, preventive and predictive maintenance programmes and mechanical maintenance procedures for mechanical equipment. Lead the maintenance, troubleshooting, repair, commissioning and replacement of mechanical static equipment and piping systems. Manages the in-house contractor and equipment vendor to execute mechanical work/improvements within the terminal to ensure safe work execution and gas supply reliability. Responsible to ensure compliance to applicable regulations/industry best practices, safe to operate, fit for purpose, maintainable and reliable for the site's modified or newly build mechanical systems (such as marine loading arm, pumps, pressure vessel, safety device, etc). Manage and ensure list of statutory equipment (lifting machine, lifting gears, pressure vessel, safety valves, etc) are timely serviced and inspected in accordance to statutory codes and compliance standards. Perform root cause analysis on equipment failure and provide recommendations to resolve technical issues which may also arise from vibration, RT, UT, oil and other analysis as part of predictive maintenance programmes. Manage and maintain historical information on static and rotating equipment such as running hours, data statistical information on all plant shut down and loss of production events caused by mechanical systems, engineering documents and review applicable codes and standards for compliance purpose. Lead technical discussions, execute and manage project schedules and cost and collaboration with external and internal stakeholders, vendors for end of life and improvement project using SLNG Management of Change (MOC) process for mechanical equipment. Liaise with external stakeholder including government agencies on approval and risk of shutdown of mechanical system that will affect gas-send out. Participate in RCA, RBI, HAZID, HAZOP, Model Review studies for mechanical packages, co-ordinates and close out Mechanical related issues. Participate in emergency response and may be assigned to a specific role and responsibility for such purpose.    REQUIREMENTS  Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical Engineering or other relevant Engineering/Technical disciplines. Candidates with professional accreditation or affiliation to professional bodies of engineers will have an advantage. At least 3 years' experience in maintenance of plant equipment, rotating machinery and static equipment, preferably in oil/petrochemical or related industries. Good knowledge and experience working on different types of in-service pipeline inspection. Knowledge of the LNG / natural gas or other hydrocarbon industries an advantage. Relevant technical experience in maintenance of mechanical/ machinery systems i.e compressor, pump, pressure vessel, hydraulic components and piping system. Conversant with local codes, regulations and standards, equipment inspection and schedule requirements. Certified in API, vibration and other analysis standards will be well regarded. The incumbent should possess the following personal attributes:   A team player who can work well in a team and with good listening skill. High self-motivation with the ability to work with minimal supervision and adapt to changing priorities and assignments. Willing to take up new challenges and responsibilities. Profile description:  GENERAL OVERVIEW   This position is part of the site Mechanical team that provides technical/maintenance support to the Operation and Project teams in LNG Terminal. He/she would be mainly responsible for overseeing the safe and reliable operations and maintenance of the Terminal assets from the Mechanical discipline perspective.   The incumbent is also responsible for establishing, reviewing and overseeing the relevant mechanical design elements of new build equipment / asset enhancement activities and implementation of minor mechanical engineering projects, while ensuring the overall mechanical integrity of the terminal.
Location: Jurong Island And Bukom, West Region

Role OverviewThe Compliance Officer is responsible for supporting the implementation and maintenance of the company’s compliance, regulatory, and governance frameworks in Singapore. This role ensures that the business complies with relevant laws, regulations, internal policies, and industry standards, with a particular focus on corporate services, CSP and ACRA regulatory requirements, AML/CFT, KYC, sanctions, data protection, and internal control processes.The successful candidate will work closely with client-service teams and group compliance functions to ensure adherence to the Group’s compliance expectations and risk appetite.Key Responsibilities1. Regulatory Compliance· Monitor and ensure compliance with relevant Singapore laws and regulations (including ACRA, CSP requirements, Companies Act, AML/CFT Notices and Guidelines, and PDPA).· Support regulatory filings, notifications, and audits.· Maintain an up-to-date understanding of regulatory changes and assess their impact on the business.2. AML/CFT, KYC & Sanctions· Perform client onboarding reviews, including KYC checks, risk assessments, sanctions screening, and verification of documentation.· Conduct ongoing monitoring, trigger event reviews, and periodic client due diligence updates.· Investigate and escalate potential red flags, suspicious transactions, or sanctions concerns.· Support preparation and submission of STRs/SARs where required.3. Internal Compliance & Governance· Assist in developing, reviewing, and implementing compliance policies, SOPs, and internal controls.· Support Group-level initiatives to standardise compliance processes across jurisdictions.· Conduct internal compliance checks and follow-up actions to ensure adherence to policies and procedures.4. Training & Advisory· Provide compliance advisory to business teams on regulatory matters, client acceptance, and risk issues.· Deliver or coordinate periodic AML/CFT and compliance training sessions for staff.· Assist in fostering a strong compliance culture consistent with the Group’s integrity standards.5. Incident Reporting & Risk Management· Support investigations into compliance breaches or complaints.· Prepare compliance reports for management, regulators, and Group governance committees.· Track remediation actions and ensure timely closure.6. Recordkeeping & Documentation· Maintain proper compliance records, KYC files, client risk assessments, and internal attestations.· Ensure documentation meets regulatory and Group standards for audit readiness.Qualifications & Experience· Bachelor’s degree in Law, Finance, Business, or related discipline.· 3–6 years of compliance experience, preferably within CSP/corporate services, professional services firm, financial services, or regulatory environment.· Strong knowledge of Singapore AML/CFT and sanctions requirements, ACRA CSP regulations, and client due diligence standards.· Experience with compliance systems, screening tools, or e-KYC platforms is an advantage.· Certification such as ACAMS, ICA, or SCTP is preferred.Competencies & Skills· High level of integrity, professionalism, and strong ethical judgment.· Excellent attention to detail and ability to manage multiple tasks.· Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.· Good communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work with cross-functional teams.· Ability to work independently while contributing to a team-oriented environment.· Fluence in Chinese speaking and writing

We are building an in-house pet relocation capability and are looking for a Pet Relocation Specialist to manage animal import and export regulatory processes end to end.
This role focuses on compliance, documentation, and coordination to ensure pets move safely and in full accordance with AVS and destination-country requirements.
Key Responsibilities
Manage animal import, export and transhipment cases from intake to approval
Prepare, submit and track AVS licence applications and related regulatory documentation
Interpret AVS regulations, rabies risk categories, and country-specific requirements
Review veterinary records, vaccination histories, microchip details and health certificates
Coordinate with AVS, veterinarians, airlines, quarantine facilities and logistics partners
Maintain accurate case files, timelines, SOPs and compliance checklists
Provide clear regulatory guidance to internal teams and clients
Monitor regulatory changes and update processes accordingly

Who This Role Is For
1–2 years of direct, hands-on experience in animal import and export regulatory approvals, licences or compliance documentation
Proven experience submitting government licence applications for animal movement
Highly organised, detail-driven, and comfortable working with formal regulatory processes
Experience with AVS, biosecurity, veterinary, logistics or regulated animal-related industries is a strong advantage

Why Join Us
Build a new in-house capability from the ground up
High ownership and clear responsibility from day one
Meaningful work supporting safe, compliant pet relocation
Opportunity to grow into a senior or lead role as the operation scales

Company description:Singapore LNG Corporation Pte Ltd (SLNG) was incorporated by the Energy Market Authority of Singapore in June 2009 to build, own and operate Singapore's very first open-access, multi-user LNG Terminal. This is a key national infrastructure that supports Singapore's energy diversification strategy and future economic development in the energy sector. With more than 95% of electricity in Singapore being generated using natural gas, the SLNG Terminal helps to enhance the country's energy security by enabling natural gas to be shipped to Singapore from anywhere in the world. It also serves as a platform to facilitate the development of new LNG-related businesses, thereby contributing to the growth of Singapore's energy industry and the creation of new job opportunities.Job description:GENERAL OVERVIEW The candidate shall be involved in managing and coordinating Risk Management and Insurance activities for the Company, as well as the Delegation of Authority within the organization. The candidate will also be involved in liaising with external consultants for Internal Audit and Fraud Risk Assessment.   ROLES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Risk Management  Manage and apply the Risk Management Frameworks including reviewing and enforcing policies, procedures, tools and methodologies. Work with various business groups on qualitative and quantitative risk management, including risk identification, risk assessment, risk analysis and control assessment. Provide support to develop and monitor the execution of risk mitigation plans. Plan and conduct corporate risk workshops annually; and project risk workshops where necessary. Provide support for the monitoring and reporting of business risks, including key risk indicators. Facilitate the preparation and update of risk registers and relevant risk reports for senior management and risk committees. Perform secretariat duties to the risk committees.    Delegation of Authority  Review and enforce the Delegation of Authority Policy. Provide advice on queries related to the application of the policy. Provide support for the monitoring and reporting of reportable items to the senior management and risk committees.    Internal Audit and Fraud Risk Assessment  Liaise with external consultant on the Internal Audit and Fraud Risk Assessment activities, including but not limited to, planning of meetings with internal stakeholders, provision of requested information, provision of clarifications to the audit / risk assessment. Provide support for the audit / risk assessment by bridging any technical knowledge or process knowledge gaps between the consultants and the internal stakeholders. Exercise professional judgement in the review of information (e.g. requested documents, clarifications, consultant reports).    Insurance  Management of annual renewal of Company's insurance policies and placement of insurance policies (where required by the business). Provide support in insurance related matters within the organization, including but not limited to, providing insurance related advices and working closely with insurance broker on ad-hoc insurance needs.    REQUIREMENTS  Degree in Finance, Accountancy, Risk Management, Business Administration or relevant discipline. Candidate shall have at least 5 years of working experience, preferably in Risk Management and/or Internal / External Audit role. Additional finance / insurance related work experience will be considered favorably. Candidates with Chartered Accountant (Singapore), or equivalent, certification will be considered favorably. Candidates from energy or asset intensive industries will be considered favorably. Candidate with prior experience in working a multi-cultural environment is an advantage. Candidate shall be proficient with MS Office applications.

Company: The Great Eastern Life Assurance Company Limited
Job Description:
This role involves establishing and managing market conduct risks through frameworks designed to evaluate the conduct and quality of sales practices. The position incorporates oversight of the Balanced Scorecard (BSC) framework, monitoring of Replacement of Policies (ROP), and Market Conduct Advisory to ensure comprehensive conduct assurance and governance across the company’s distribution activities.



·       Oversee the governance and integrity of the BSC assessment process in accordance with MAS’ Balanced Scorecard Framework.

·       Continuously monitor BSC trends, assess representatives or agencies with elevated risk profiles, and escalate systemic conduct risks appropriately.

·       Govern and validate ROP reviews in alignment with LIA Standards for Distributors on Deterrence of Undesirable Switching – Replacement of Policies.

·       Identify potential policy churn or mis-selling patterns and escalate for investigative or disciplinary follow-up.

·       Review new or updated MAS and LIA regulatory requirements, translate them into internal policies, and advise business units on market conduct related matters.

·       Lead compliance monitoring / assurance programs on targeted areas of market conduct

·       Serves as primary liaison with regulators for matters relating to dialogues, inspections, reviews, and the co-ordination of responses related to market conduct.

·       Management of Fair Dealing Program across the Group in alignment with MAS’s Guidelines on Fair Dealing.

·       Ensure accurate, regular and timely reports to Management, Board and/or regulators.

·       Collaborate cross-functionally with Investigation, Fitness & Propriety, and Compliance Surveillance teams.



·       Bachelor’s degree and preferably with 10 years of prior experience in compliance, audit, or sales conduct roles in the insurance or financial services sector.

·       Comprehensive understanding of FAA, FAR, BSC regulations, strong knowledge of MAS Guidelines such as Fair Dealing, and related LIA requirements.

·       Professional certifications such as CMFAS.

·       High ethical standards and professional integrity.

·       Strong analytical and investigative skills.

·       Effective stakeholder management and communication.

 The Munich Re LH2 Regional Structured Solutions team is a dynamic and high-performing team renowned for designing and delivering bespoke non-traditional reinsurance solutions (financial reinsurance) tailored to the unique needs of clients across the Asia Pacific, Middle East, and Africa regions. We are looking for an Actuarial Analyst to take on a pivotal role in the in-force management of financial reinsurance within these regions.
This position offers an exciting opportunity to advance your actuarial career by mastering the complexities of financial reinsurance. While collaborating with diverse stakeholders, you will apply your technical expertise in data management, monitoring, and valuation to ensure seamless in-force management operations of LH2 financial reinsurance. Beyond technical excellence, this role allows you to develop a well-rounded skillset by enhancing your communication, stakeholder management, and project leadership abilities. Join us in a fast-paced, results-oriented environment where you can make a meaningful impact and thrive as part of a global leader in financial reinsurance.
Your Role
Data management

Collaborate with Structured Solutions local teams to ensure accurate and timely receipt of client data for in-force management of LH2 financial reinsurance
Conduct timely checks and analysis to ensure data integrity and reasonableness

Monitoring

Support the monitoring and performance analysis of LH2 financial reinsurance, identifying trends and opportunities for optimization
Prepare regular monitoring reports with actionable insights to support informed decision-making by management

Valuation and Reporting

Collaborate with valuation teams, accounting teams, Structured Solutions local teams and central units to deliver inputs for quarterly and annual reporting under Solvency II, IFRS, and local statutory reporting frameworks for LH2 financial reinsurance
Identify keys areas of improvement, streamline and automate processes for valuation and reporting processes

Infrastructure Management

Support the design, maintenance, and enhancement (including automation)  of the monitoring and reporting infrastructures
Support in ensuring that guidelines, standards, frameworks, tools, and templates for in force management of LH2 financial reinsurance are up-to-date and well-documented

Deal Support
Provide analytical and technical support for the structuring, pricing, risk analysis, approval and execution of new or modified financial reinsurance 
Your Profile

Between 1 to 3 years of actuarial experience in direct insurance, reinsurance, or consulting
Fellow or good progress towards qualification with a recognised actuarial body (FSA, FIA, FIAA, or equivalent)
Strong technical and analytical skills, logical and concise in approach and demonstrate attention to detail
Highly motivated self-starter with a strong sense of team ethics
Proficiency in data processing and database structuring tools such as Power BI, MS excel (can perform complex functions), and SQL
Knowledge of Python is an advantage

Our Benefits

Flexible and hybrid working arrangement
Generous annual leave allowance
Comprehensive dental, life and health insurance coverage

This includes developing, reviewing and refining business rules, system logic, and process flow to ensure operational efficiency and alignment with policy intent.Participate in developing strategic product and system roadmap to support evolving policy changes and operational needs, and support the prioritisation of system enhancements, working with vendors and stakeholders to plan delivery timelines and resource allocationSupport project management for system enhancements, such as developing and monitoring the overall project schedule, reviewing testing plans and scenarios (e.g. for User Acceptance Testing) comprehensively in relation to business objectives, executing and overseeing progress of testing, as well as conducting post-implementation monitoring. Company: Consortium for Clinical Research and Innovation, Singapore
Job Description: OverviewThe Consortium for Clinical Research and Innovation, Singapore (CRIS) brings together seven national R&D, clinical translation and service programmes to advance clinical research and innovation for Singapore, and establish important capabilities for a future-ready healthcare system.The Business Entities under CRIS include:Singapore Clinical Research Institute (SCRI)National Health Innovation Centre (NHIC)Advanced Cell Therapy and Research Institute, Singapore (ACTRIS)Precision Health Research, Singapore (PRECISE)Singapore Translational Cancer Consortium (STCC)Cardiovascular Disease National Collaborative Enterprise (CADENCE)Singapore Medical Foundation AI model (SIMFONI)Together, CRIS makes a positive difference to Singapore patients and researchers by ensuring that these clinical research platforms and programmes are at the cutting edge of capability development and innovation. If you are as passionate as we are in clinical trials and research, we want you!National Health Innovation Centre SingaporeThe National Health Innovation Centre Singapore (NHIC) is a nationally-appointed Innovation and Enterprise Programme Office that works with Public Health Institutions across Singapore to identify and commercialise promising clinical innovations. We fund the translation and development of medical innovations from Singapore’s clinical sector, and provide strategic guidance and connection to industry partners, accelerating the pathway to impact in healthcare. NHIC programmes catalyse the translation of clinical innovations towards commercially viable products that benefit patients and healthcare. Established since 2014, we have supported many projects and companies in the development and implementation of innovative medical technologies and services, improving the standard of healthcare in Singapore and beyond. NHIC is part of the Consortium for Clinical Research and Innovation Singapore (CRIS).What you will be working onWe are looking for a dynamic and experienced individual to fill the role of Assistant Director, Innovation & Healthcare Transformation at NHIC.Your key role would be to lead Clinical Adoption and Healthcare Transformation initiatives at NHIC, including working with the Ministry of Health (MOH) and Public Healthcare Clusters (PHCs) to take regulatory-approved health technologies from early-stage solutions into real-world clinical practice in Singapore and beyond. The portfolio will see you getting involved in digital integration (where applicable), cost-effectiveness assessments of technologies, procurement and potential subvention to enable scale-up across Singapore for long-term adoption and financial sustainability.As part of the team, you will also support NHIC’s suite of innovation, development and commercialisation schemes. This includes being a Cluster Lead for one of the local PHCs to facilitate development, adoption and commercialisation of early innovation.
